---
title: "Generate a QC report"
description: "A standalone HTML quality-control report to choose filter thresholds."
categories: [openpipeline_qc]
order: 20
---

[`openpipeline_qc`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_qc}<span class="swatch openpipeline_qc"></span>

The **`openpipeline_qc`** package renders a standalone HTML quality-control report
from an [ingested](ingest-10x.qmd) `.h5mu`, count and feature distributions and
mitochondrial and ribosomal fractions, so you can pick sensible cutoffs before
[sample processing](process-samples.qmd).

## Options

| Workflow | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`generate_qc_report`](../reference/openpipeline_qc/v0.3.0/workflows/generate_qc_report.qmd) | Any ingested `.h5mu`; you name the ingestion method the data came from |

For spatial data, use the spatial ingestion methods, see
[Spatial QC report generation](qc-report-spatial.qmd).

## Example: report for a cohort

For example, to render one report across two ingested samples, list both inputs
and name the ingestion method they came from (`cellranger_multi` here):

```yaml
# params.yaml
id: cohort_01
input:
  - results/ingestion/sample_A.h5mu
  - results/ingestion/sample_B.h5mu
ingestion_method: cellranger_multi
output_qc_report: qc_report.html
publish_dir: results/
```

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline_qc.git \
  -r v0.3.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/generate_qc_report/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

## Key choices

- **Ingestion method** is required: name the method the data came from
  (`cellranger_multi`, `cellranger`, `bd_rhapsody`, ...); for spatial input see
  the spatial guide.
- The package also exposes `ingestion_qc/detect_ingestion_method` and
  `ingestion_qc/generate_html` as separate components if you want to compute
  metrics and render as distinct steps.
- Read the report to choose the `rna_min_counts` / `rna_max_counts` /
  `max_fraction_mito` thresholds you'll pass to
  [sample processing](process-samples.qmd).

## Output

A self-contained `qc_report.html` you open in a browser. It doesn't modify the
data, it informs the thresholds you set next in
[sample processing](process-samples.qmd).
